purpose: Declares architectural channels that M1 (cross-channel comparator) watches. Each channel has an expected_max_interval_seconds; if mtime falls behind by more than this AND the channel is active, M1 raises a fault. Escalation tier determines routing.

channels.name: daily_log_today
channels.notes: Most cross-cutting active signal. Stale today-file means daemon main loop is stuck OR all scheduled work has gone silent. Cross-correlate with kg_extraction_run.log to discriminate.

channels.name: tool_audit_jsonl
channels.notes: A115 channel - currently silent in production due to Windows hook-dispatcher regression. Daemon-layer replication queued post-T1.A; until then, this channel is expected silent and M1 should NOT escalate solely on its silence (override expected_active_when to never until replication ships).

channels.name: kg_extraction_run_log
carries: per-file extraction progress during a KG run
channels.notes: Only active during a kg_extract_corpus.py run. PID liveness check via kg_extraction_run.json is the gate; stale log mtime with live PID means extraction is stuck. Live PID + healthy log = healthy run. No live PID = inactive and silence is expected.

channels.name: handoff_md
channels.notes: Updated at session end / major work. Stale beyond 24h means no session has closed cleanly. Informational, not urgent.

channels.name: kg_index_db
channels.notes: Derived from kg_corpus_extraction.jsonl. Stale >7d means the index is out of sync with the canonical JSONL. Self-healable via rebuild script.

channels.name: monitor_m7_heartbeat
carries: M7 drift-mirror daemon-layer replication liveness (A115 fix path A)
channels.notes: M7 replicates the broken Claude Code drift_mirror hook (A115 fix). Daemon-side polling at 10-min cadence ensures essay mirror parity regardless of Claude Code hook firing.

channels.name: monitor_m8_heartbeat
carries: M8 tool-audit-shadow daemon-layer replication liveness (A115 fix path B)
channels.notes: M8 shadows the broken Claude Code post_tool_log hook by ingesting coordination.json activity_feed and writing tool_audit_shadow.jsonl. Complementary to (not replacement for) the hook; both signals are cross-correlated by M1.

channels.name: monitor_m1_heartbeat
carries: M1 cross-channel comparator's own liveness signal
channels.notes: M6 watches THIS file. If M1 itself goes silent, M6 escalates. Mandatory bidirectional heartbeat per integrated plan.

escalation_tiers.critical: Telegram-Clayton immediately. Reserved for things that genuinely require attention (monitor process death, daemon death, total channel silence).
escalation_tiers.high: Daily summary surface. Includes cross-correlation anomalies that suggest substrate issues.
escalation_tiers.medium: Weekly summary surface. Single-channel transient anomalies; usually self-resolve.

discipline_notes: Most events should be log-only. Telegram-Clayton interruptions are reserved for the critical tier.
Channels marked expected_active_when must be checked against their gate signal before raising silence faults.
Self-healing where applicable: attempt the documented command first; only escalate if it fails.
